sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jack, a 30-year-old male, presents with a one-week history of neck pain following a car accident.
He reports that he was hit in the back, his neck went forward, and his head went forward quickly.
He describes the pain as a 7 out of 10, which is exacerbated by movement of his neck.
The pain is localized to the back of his neck and has not been relieved by ibuprofen.
He reports occasional headaches but denies any hearing or visual disturbances.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of playing football years ago without any neck-related issues.
He denies any previous medical conditions, treatments, or encounters that may be relevant to his current presentation.
Review of systems: The patient denies any symptoms in other organs that are relevant to his initial presentation.
Current medications: The patient is currently taking ibuprofen as needed.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient exhibits positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, pain with flexion, severe pain with extension, and positive pain with rotation.
He is able to turn his neck from side to side with some pain, but is unable to touch his ear to his shoulders.
Test Results: The patient's x-ray results show no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I believe the patient has a neck strain, likely caused by whiplash from the car accident.
Problem list: Neck strain; whiplash; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600 milligrams every 6 to 8 hours for anti-inflammatory purposes.
I also prescribed Flexeril 10 milligrams every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I instructed the patient to be conservative with his neck movement and avoid straining it.
I ordered an MRI to rule out any underlying conditions, such as a concussion.
I will refer the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor once the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up to discuss the results of the MRI and further management.
